title: ～よりほかない
titleEn: Express that there is no other choice; 'have no choice but', 'cannot do anything but'.
structure: Verb-dictionary form + よりほかない
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + よりほかない
explanation: Express that there is no other choice; 'have no choice but', 'cannot do anything but'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～よりほかない grammar point is used to express that there is no other choice or alternative in a given situation. It can be translated as 'have no choice but to...', 'nothing but...', or 'cannot do anything but...'.

title: ～わけがない
titleEn: Expresses 'there’s no way' or 'it’s impossible', indicating a high degree of certainty.
structure: Verb-casual + わけがない, い-Adjective + わけがない, な-Adjective + なわけがない, Noun + のわけがない
structureEn: Verb-casual + わけがない, い-Adjective + わけがない, な-Adjective + なわけがない, Noun + のわけがない
explanation: Expresses 'there’s no way' or 'it’s impossible', indicating a high degree of certainty. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～わけがない grammar point is used to express that something is highly unlikely or impossible. It can be translated as 'there's no way' or 'it's impossible'.

title: ～わけだ
titleEn: Express a logical conclusion or reason; 'it means', 'no wonder', 'the reason is'.
structure: Verb-casual + わけだ, い-Adjective + わけだ, な-Adjective + なわけだ, Noun + のわけだ
structureEn: Verb-casual + わけだ, い-Adjective + わけだ, な-Adjective + なわけだ, Noun + のわけだ
explanation: Express a logical conclusion or reason; 'it means', 'no wonder', 'the reason is'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～わけだ grammar point is used to express a logical conclusion or reason. It can be translated as 'it means...', 'no wonder...', or 'that explains why...'.

title: ～わけではない
titleEn: Not necessarily; it doesn't mean that ~.
structure: Verb-plain + わけではない, い-Adjective + わけではない, な-Adjective + だわけではない, Noun + だわけではない
structureEn: Verb-plain + わけではない, い-Adjective + わけではない, な-Adjective + だわけではない, Noun + だわけではない
explanation: Not necessarily; it doesn't mean that ~. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～わけではない grammar point is used to express that something is not necessarily the case or 'it doesn’t mean that...' It negates a broad assumption or generalization.

title: ～わけにはいかない
titleEn: Expresses that one cannot (or must not) do something due to responsibility, social pressure, etc.
structure: Verb-dictionary form + わけにはいかない
structureEn: Verb-dictionary form + わけにはいかない
explanation: Expresses that one cannot (or must not) do something due to responsibility, social pressure, etc. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～わけにはいかない grammar point is used to express that you cannot afford to do something or are not allowed to do it. It often implies a sense of responsibility, moral/social obligation, or external pressure that prevents you from doing it.

title: ～をきっかけに
titleEn: Indicates that something serves as a trigger or starting point for another action/event.
explanation: Indicates that something serves as a trigger or starting point for another action/event. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「～をきっかけに」 is used with a noun to show that an event, thing, or situation became a trigger or motive that led to a new development or action. It translates as 'taking ... as an opportunity,' 'due to ...,' or 'inspired by ...'.

title: ～を～として
titleEn: Express 'as', 'in the capacity of', or 'taking something as...'.
structure: Noun1 + を + Noun2 + として
structureEn: Noun1 + を + Noun2 + として
explanation: Express 'as', 'in the capacity of', or 'taking something as...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar pattern 「～を～として」 is used with nouns to indicate taking one thing 'as' or 'in the role of' another. It shows how a certain noun is being viewed or utilized.

title: ～を中心に
titleEn: Indicates that something is centered around or focused on a main topic or area.
explanation: Indicates that something is centered around or focused on a main topic or area. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「～を中心に」 is used with nouns to show that an action or situation is mainly or primarily about the specified noun. It can be translated as 'centered on', 'focusing on', or 'with ... as the main focus'.
